questions: The chorion is formed by the *trophoblast* and extraembryonic somatic mesoderm. The part facing the decidua basalis with proliferating villi is called chorion *frondosum*.
The primary source of amniotic fluid after 16 weeks is fetal *urine*, and the fetus removes fluid by *swallowing* about 500–600 mL per day.
Normal amniotic fluid volume at 36 weeks is approximately *800–1000/800-1000* mL. Excess fluid greater than 2 litres is called *polyhydramnios*.
The human placenta is *haemochorial:maternal blood bathes chorionic villi*, meaning maternal blood directly bathes the chorionic *villi*.
Potter sequence results from *oligohydramnios* caused by bilateral renal *agenesis*, leading to pulmonary hypoplasia and limb deformities.
